channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: After Replacing TeamPCP Malware, 'PCPJack' Steals Cloud Secrets
item.description: PCPJack makes innovative use of parquet files for stealthy, pre-validated target discovery as it canvasses multiple cloud environments....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Has CISA Finally Found Its New Leader in Tom Parker?
item.description: Dark Reading investigates rumors that Tom Parker, a board room "operator" and longtime cyber exec, could be next in line to take over CISA....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New PCPJack worm steals credentials, cleans TeamPCP infections
item.description: A new malware framework called PCPJack is stealing credentials from exposed cloud infrastructure while actively removing TeamPCP's access to the systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Australia warns of ClickFix attacks pushing Vidar Stealer malware
item.description: The Australian Cyber Security Center (ACSC) is warning organizations of an ongoing malware campaign using the ClickFix social engineering technique to distribute the Vidar Stealer info-stealing malwa...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ivanti EPMM CVE-2026-6973 RCE Under Active Exploitation Grants Admin-Level Access
item.description: Ivanti is warning that a new security flaw impacting Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) has been explored in limited attacks in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-6973 (CVSS score: 7.2), ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: PCPJack Credential Stealer Exploits 5 CVEs to Spread Worm-Like Across Cloud Systems
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new credential theft framework dubbed PCPJack that targets exposed cloud infrastructure and ousts any artifacts linked to TeamPCP from the environ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ivanti warns of new EPMM flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
item.description: Ivanti warned customers today to patch a high-severity remote code execution vulnerability in Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Browser Is Breaking Your DLP: How Data Slips Past Modern Controls
item.description: Your security controls aren't failing, they're missing where most of today's work actually happens. Keep Aware shows how browser activity like copy/paste and AI prompts bypass traditional protections....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero"...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Americans sentenced for running 'laptop farms' for North Korea
item.description: Two U.S. nationals were sentenced to 18 months in prison each for operating so-called laptop farms that helped North Korean IT workers fraudulently obtain remote employment at nearly 70 American compa...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: PAN-OS RCE Exploit Under Active Use Enabling Root Access and Espionage
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has disclosed that threat actors may have attempted to unsuccessfully exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw as early as April 9, 2026. The vulnerability in question is...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 'TrustFall' Convention Exposes Claude Code Execution Risk
item.description: Malicious repositories can trigger code execution in Claude Code, Cursor CLI, Gemini CLI, and CoPilot CLI with minimal or no user interaction, thanks to skimpy warning dialogs....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: World's First AI-Driven Cyberattack Couldn't Breach OT Systems
item.description: The most sophisticated AI-integrated campaign to date hit a brick wall in the form of a SCADA login screen....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Crypto gang member gets 6.5 years for role in $230 million heist
item.description: A 20-year-old California man was sentenced to 78 months in prison for serving as a home invader and money launderer in a criminal ring that stole over $250 million in cryptocurrency. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Webinar: Why modern attacks require both security and recovery
item.description: Modern attacks don't stop at initial compromise. This webinar explores why security and recovery must work together to reduce downtime and improve resilience. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Edge Plaintext Passwords, ICS 0-Days, Patch-or-Die Alerts and 25+ New Stories
item.description: Bad week. Turns out the easiest way to get hacked in 2026 is still the same old garbage: shady packages, fake apps, forgotten DNS junk, scam ads, and stolen logins getting dumped into Discord channels...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Palo Alto Networks firewall zero-day exploited for nearly a month
item.description: Palo Alto Networks warned customers that suspected state-sponsored hackers have been exploiting a critical-severity PAN-OS firewall zero-day vulnerability for nearly a month. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Day Zero Readiness: The Operational Gaps That Break Incident Response
item.description: Having an incident response retainer, or even a pre-approved external incident response firm, is not the same as being ready for an incident. A retainer means someone will answer the phone. Operationa...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fake Claude AI website delivers new 'Beagle' Windows malware
item.description: A fake version for the Claude AI website offers a malicious Claude-Pro Relay download that pushes a previously undocumented backdoor for Windows named Beagle. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: PyPI Packages Deliver ZiChatBot Malware via Zulip APIs on Windows and Linux
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered three packages on the Python Package Index (PyPI) repository that are designed to stealthily deliver a previously unknown malware family called ZiChatBot on W...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: vm2 Node.js Library Vulnerabilities Enable Sandbox Escape and Arbitrary Code Execution
item.description: A dozen critical security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in the vm2 Node.js library that could be exploited by bad actors to break out of the sandbox and execute arbitrary code on susceptible sys...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers abuse Google ads for GoDaddy ManageWP login phishing
item.description: A phishing campaign delivered through Google sponsored search results is targeting credentials for ManageWP, GoDaddy's platform for managing fleets of WordPress websites. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Yet Another Way to Bypass Google Chrome's Encryption Protection
item.description: Authors of the VoidStealer Trojan uncovered a way to get around Google's App-Bound Encryption (ABE), opening the door to infostealers....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Instructure Breach Exposes Schools' Vendor Dependence
item.description: ShinyHunters' attack on Instructure, which owns the widely used Canvas learning management system (LMS), carries big questions about the trust educational institutions put into their vendors....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Mirai-Based xlabs_v1 Botnet Exploits ADB to Hijack IoT Devices for DDoS Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have exposed a new Mirai-derived botnet that self-identifies as xlabs_v1 and targets internet-exposed devices running Android Debug Bridge (ADB) to enlist them in a network c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critical vm2 sandbox bug lets attackers execute code on hosts
item.description: A critical vulnerability in the popular Node.js sandboxing library vm2 allows escaping the sandbox and executing arbitrary code on the host system. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Cisco DoS flaw requires manual reboot to revive devices
item.description: Cisco patched a Crosswork Network Controller and Network Services Orchestrator denial-of-service vulnerability that requires manually rebooting targeted systems for recovery. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DAEMON Tools devs confirm breach, release malware-free version
item.description: Disc Soft Limited, the maker of DAEMON Tools Lite, confirmed that the software had been trojanized in a supply chain attack and released a new, malware-free version. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Why ransomware attacks succeed even when backups exist
item.description: Backups don't fail because they're missing, they fail because attackers destroy them first. Acronis explains how ransomware targets backup systems before encryption, leaving no path to recovery. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: MuddyWater hackers use Chaos ransomware as a decoy in attacks
item.description: The MuddyWater Iranian hackers disguised their operations as a Chaos ransomware attack, relying on Microsoft Teams social engineering to gain access and establish persistence. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
